title: PlatformDepartment enum seed
description: Canonical department taxonomy for the Stackra internal-workforce plane. Consumed at boot by `PlatformDepartment` enum registration + by the `valid_department` validation rule. Adding a new department here requires a schema migration (the department column is an enum type in Postgres) AND a coordinated update to the settings-module's default `notifications.department_channel_map`. Reordering rows is free — sort_order drives the picker UI, not the enum discriminator.
columns_note: Fields on each row: `key` (enum member name, snake_case, stable identifier for FKs), `display_name` (human-readable label), `slug` (URL-safe form for API filters), `sort_order` (picker sort ascending), `default_notifications_channel` (Slack channel or similar for department-wide broadcasts), `on_call_rotation` (whether this department participates in the on-call rotation by default).

departments.description: Day-to-day platform operations, incident response, tenant support triage. Backbone of the platform-admin plane.
Software engineering across backend, frontend, and platform. Participates in on-call for the systems they own.
Customer support agents — primary consumers of the `platform.impersonate` entitlement for tenant-side troubleshooting.
Contracts, regulatory response, HR-adjacent legal review.
Security engineering, incident response, threat modeling. Primary holders of `platform.security_review` entitlement.
Data protection officers + privacy compliance. Primary consumers of `platform.audit_read` entitlement for compliance-review workflows.
People operations. Owns the `platform.platform_users.hire` + `.offboard` + `.update` permissions on employment metadata.

notes: The `dpo` department is distinct from `legal` — in EU jurisdictions, the DPO role must be operationally independent of legal counsel, so staffers wear separate hats.

`on_call_rotation` is a HINT for the default rotation membership; individual PlatformUsers can opt in/out via `on_call_rotation_key` regardless of department. The hint drives the default suggestion in the UI.

Adding a new department requires: (1) schema migration to extend the Postgres enum, (2) update this file, (3) update settings default `notifications.department_channel_map`, (4) coordinate with the notifications module to provision the new Slack channel.
